meta.title: Seizure Freedom after Laser Ablation (SLAH) — Ordinal Score
meta.authors: Dickey AS, Bullinger KL, Grogan D, et al.
meta.journal: Annals of Clinical and Translational Neurology
meta.outcome: Predicted chance of seizure freedom 1 year after stereotactic laser amygdalohippocampotomy (SLAH) for mesial temporal lobe epilepsy
meta.clinical_utility: An 8-point ordinal clinical score (Dickey et al. 2024) that predicts the chance of seizure freedom one year after stereotactic laser amygdalohippocampotomy (SLAH / laser ablation) for mesial temporal lobe epilepsy. One point is awarded for each of eight features concordant with mesial temporal lobe epilepsy; a higher score means a higher predicted chance of seizure freedom.
meta.cff_info:
What this shows. An ordinal clinical score (Dickey et al., Ann Clin Transl Neurol 2024) predicting the chance of seizure freedom one year after laser ablation (SLAH) for mesial temporal lobe epilepsy, from 101 patients at Emory University. One point is given for each of eight features typical of mesial temporal lobe epilepsy (0–8 total); more concordant features → higher predicted seizure freedom.

Key findings

  • Seizure-freedom chance rises steadily with the score: ~6% at 0 up to ~89% at 8.
  • A score ≥6 predicts a good outcome (≥71% seizure free); ≤4 predicts a poor outcome (≤41%).
  • The ordinal score out-predicted MTS alone or a full multivariable model (AUC 0.70 vs 0.54–0.62).

Lateralized findings (IEDs, ictal onset, MTS, PET) score a point only when they are unitemporal and ipsilateral to the planned surgery; bitemporal, contralateral or non-localizing findings score 0. This is a demonstration tool, not a substitute for multidisciplinary presurgical evaluation.

predictors.name: History of infantile febrile seizures
predictors.assess: A documented history of febrile seizures in infancy/early childhood. Present = 1 point.

predictors.name: Onset of epilepsy ≤ 16 years
predictors.assess: Age at epilepsy onset 16 years or younger. Yes = 1 point.

predictors.name: Rare or no generalized tonic-clonic seizures
predictors.assess: Few or no secondarily generalized tonic-clonic seizures. Yes = 1 point.

predictors.name: No auditory, vertiginous or visual aura
predictors.assess: Absence of an auditory, vertiginous or visual aura (these suggest non-mesial/neocortical onset). Absent such aura = 1 point.

predictors.name: Ipsilateral temporal interictal discharges (IEDs)
predictors.assess: Interictal epileptiform discharges on scalp EEG. Score 1 only if the finding is unitemporal AND ipsilateral to the planned surgery side; bitemporal, extratemporal, contralateral or non-localizing findings score 0. (Non-localizing or no interictals = 0.)

predictors.name: Ipsilateral temporal ictal onset
predictors.assess: Scalp/intracranial ictal onset localized to the temporal lobe. Score 1 only if the finding is unitemporal AND ipsilateral to the planned surgery side; bitemporal, extratemporal, contralateral or non-localizing findings score 0.

predictors.name: Ipsilateral mesial temporal sclerosis (MTS) on MRI
predictors.assess: MRI evidence of mesial temporal sclerosis. Score 1 only if the finding is unitemporal AND ipsilateral to the planned surgery side; bitemporal, extratemporal, contralateral or non-localizing findings score 0.

predictors.name: Ipsilateral temporal PET hypometabolism
predictors.assess: FDG-PET hypometabolism in the temporal lobe. Score 1 only if the finding is unitemporal AND ipsilateral to the planned surgery side; bitemporal, extratemporal, contralateral or non-localizing findings score 0.

model.panel_title: Chance of seizure freedom at 1 year
model.viz_info: Each concordant feature adds one point. The waterfall (left) starts at the baseline chance of seizure freedom and steps up as each feature raises the predicted seizure-freedom; the donut (right) shows the features as wedges summing to the predicted chance of seizure freedom in the centre. Higher score = better predicted outcome.

provenance.source: Dickey 2024 Ann Clin Transl Neurol Table 3 (PMID39001603, doi 10.1002/acn3.52146)
provenance.notes: 8 binary predictors (1pt each, 0-8) from Table 3 / Methods; seizure-freedom %/score from Table 3 (logistic-regression prediction, matches Fig 3 observed). Lateralized vars scored 1 only if unitemporal+ipsilateral. Extracted 2026-07-07.
